strategy: Stack accuracy. At the end of Round 1 she puts a -50% accuracy Darkness aura on every wizard for 99 Rounds, so pack Sun accuracy enchants and accuracy gear to avoid fizzles.
Play to out-damage, not out-heal. Doom and Gloom is up from the start of combat, so your heals are weak.
Protect your Feint with Indemnity. Feinting Nyx makes her Shadow Cleanse Ward and strip every trap off herself, and Mass Feint and pet spells trigger it too.
Funnel blades to a hitter rather than buffing yourself. Casting on yourself only triggers her harmless interrupt nudging you to buff allies.
Manage pips and Health against her Dark Nova, Dark Fiend, Dark Shepherd, and pip-stealing Natural Attack.
At 41,100 Health this is a team fight: one hitter with accuracy stacked, supports blading and Feinting under Indemnity, and a healer working around Doom and Gloom.
cheatNotes: Doom and Gloom is up from the start of combat, cutting all healing, so out-damage rather than out-heal.
At the end of Round 1 she puts a -50% accuracy Darkness aura on every wizard for 99 Rounds, so expect fizzles unless you stack accuracy enchants and gear.
She attacks with immediate Dark Nova, Dark Fiend, and Dark Shepherd, or a Natural Attack that also steals your pips.
If you cast a spell on yourself she interrupts to hint you should buff allies instead; no real effect, but funnel blades to your hitter anyway.
Feinting Nyx makes her Shadow Cleanse Ward and strip every trap off herself.
Protect the Feint with Indemnity to beat that cleanse.
Mass Feint (+75%) also triggers the cleanse.
Pet-cast spells trigger it as well, so watch your pet's Feint and trap casts.
